claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch system implements full provider-adapter boundary control for Claude/Codex/custom agents with post-execution snapshot auditing, comprehensive test coverage showing dual-gate execution authorization and provider isolation, and active runtime evidence generation in .yolo/demand/evidence/.

evidence.why: Independent verification of boundary tool allowlist definition.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot uses git ls-files or walkFiles, computes SHA256 digests, excludes .git/node_modules/dist/coverage/.next/.cache
evidence.why: Confirmed snapshot mechanism matches explorer claim.

evidence.summary: Dual-gate checks: execute=true AND allowAgentDispatch=true required before spawning agents
evidence.why: Independent verification of execution authorization logic.

evidence.summary: boundary.mutation = 'violated' when boundaryChanges.length > 0, BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers added
allowFullAgentTools normalization and agentToolProfile→boundary/research/full conversion confirmed
buildProviderInvocation constructs Claude CLI with --allowedTools CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS, --no-session-persistence
evidence.why: Verified translation layer from boundary config to CLI arguments.

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt spawns child process, pipes prompt, captures stdout/stderr, handles timeout
Test simulates agent write outside allowed root, asserts boundary.violated and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker
evidence.why: Critical test evidence for boundary enforcement.

evidence.summary: Tests verify full tool profile requires allowFullAgentTools, defaults to boundary without authorization
evidence.why: Confirmed tool profile gating tests exist and pass.

evidence.summary: Current dispatch contains explorer.json (11961 bytes), protocol shows read-only intent
evidence.why: Verified evidence artifact structure matches schema.

evidence.summary: All 11 tests pass in 966ms: plan, dry-run, dual-gate, provider isolation, tool profile, boundary enforcement, conflict handling
evidence.why: Independent test run confirms comprehensive coverage.

assumptions: CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS array is complete for Claude Code's current tool set (no independent verification against Claude Code documentation)
Boundary snapshot detects all relevant mutation types (symlinks, permissions, metadata not explicitly tested)
Real Claude -p agents respect claude_settings permissions.allow in production (tests use mock spawns)
Provider isolation prevents cross-provider model/state leakage (no explicit integration tests)
Evidence agents return valid JSON schema in production (normalization exists but edge cases untested)
risks: If CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS is incomplete, agents may access unexpected tools not caught by tests
If boundary snapshot has false negatives (misses mutations), project files can be corrupted without triggering blockers
If real Claude agents ignore --allowedTools in production, boundary enforcement fails despite passing tests
If provider isolation leaks state between providers, unexpected behavior could occur
If evidence agents return malformed JSON, normalization may lose critical data or mask failures
missing: Independent verification that CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS matches Claude Code's current available tool set
Runtime logs showing real Claude agents respecting boundary settings (vs test mocks)
Integration tests confirming provider isolation prevents cross-provider leakage
Evidence that boundary snapshot detects symlinks, permissions, and metadata changes
Production dispatch logs showing boundary violations caught and blocked
Cross-check of recent agent outputs showing read-only protocol adherence

result.notes: Independent source code reads, test execution, and artifact inspection confirm explorer's factual claims about boundary control implementation, snapshot auditing, dual-gate execution, provider isolation, and runtime evidence generation. All 11 tests pass. However, critical assumptions remain about production behavior: real Claude agents respecting boundary settings, CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS completeness, and snapshot detecting all mutation types. These are not implementation gaps but verification gaps—tests use mock spawns, so production behavior remains partially unverified. The demand's acceptance criteria specifically requires cross-check verification before PRD readiness, which this agent now provides.
